rationale: File boundaries are clean for the parallel pair: Bead 1 (internal/config) and Bead 2 (internal/panel) are package-disjoint, share no key_file_paths, and have no import edge either direction (config.go references internal/panel only in comments; grep for internal/panel in internal/config finds no import — leaf invariant holds). No shared produced state, so they are genuinely parallel. The single file touched by 2 beads is .mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md (Beads 2 and 3); that overlap rides the already-real B2 -> B3 edge, so Bead 3 lands strictly after Bead 2 and edits the doc atop Bead 2's merged version — no parallel-write hazard, no false dependency, no deepened DAG. No file is touched by >=3 beads. Bead 3's two parent edges are both genuine compile-time: it calls Bead 1's new resolvers (PanelGateAdvisoryDefault/PanelGateExpectedReviewers/PanelGateApproveThresholdExpr/PanelGateReviewerSlots) and reads Bead 2's new panel.Panel.Gate (verified at complete.go:384 and cmd/mindspec/config.go) — neither symbol exists without its parent. The Reviewer.Count->*int change's only external consumer is cmd/mindspec/config.go:149 (Bead 3's own file, %%d-formatted, still compiles); report.go's r.Count is a different struct (reports, not reviewers) — no hidden edge. Parallelism 0.67 (2/3 roots) and longest-chain-2 are accurate. Frontmatter key_file_paths match each bead's body.

findings.note: Only cross-bead file overlap: .mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md is in both Bead 2 (gate-field schema note + leaf-invariant reaffirmation) and Bead 3 (gate-aware advisory selection rule). This is not a merge-signal violation (that trips at >=3 beads) and cannot conflict because B2 -> B3 is a real dependency edge — Bead 3 appends after Bead 2's version is already in the tree. Distinct sections, distinct concerns. Non-blocking; noted for completeness.

Reviewer.Count -> *int leaves go build green at the Bead-1-only state: the sole external consumer, cmd/mindspec/config.go:149, is %d-formatted (fmt accepts *int at compile time) and is rewritten by Bead 3 anyway; cmd/mindspec/report.go's r.Count is an unrelated reports struct. No untracked file is forced to change by this type change.
